summary:
The test passes. 

**The needle:** In `Product.java`, the `hashCode()` method was using `category.hashCode()` instead of `sku.hashCode()`. Since there are only 10 distinct categories, all 10,000 products were being hashed into just ~10 buckets, causing massive hash collisions and O(n) lookup degradation. The fix was simply changing `category.hashCode()` to `sku.hashCode()`, which properly distributes items across buckets (max chain length dropped from ~1000 to 4).
